skill.name: scvi-tools
skill.description: Guides single-cell omics analysis with scvi-tools, including scRNA-seq, scATAC-seq, CITE-seq, spatial transcriptomics, differential expression, and probabilistic model workflows.
skill.summary: Analyze single-cell omics data with scvi-tools model guidance and workflow references.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are documentation and scientific terminology false positives. The skill contains markdown examples for Python, installation, local model persistence, and official scvi-tools links, with no auto-executing scripts or prompt injection evidence. Publication risk is low because users may copy commands that install packages or write local files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings occur in markdown prose, inline code, and fenced Python examples. These files are documentation and do not execute commands when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visibly markdown fences or scientific examples, not Ruby source or runtime shell execution. No executable wrapper or script was found in the scanned skill files.

low_findings.title: Scientific Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings map to scientific model names and parameters such as DestVI, variational inference, and hidden-layer terminology. They are not cryptographic APIs or security-sensitive algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is single-cell modeling documentation, not encryption, hashing, signing, or credential handling. The labels appear caused by substring matches against domain vocabulary.

low_findings.title: User-Run Installation and Local Persistence Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples that users may copy to install scvi-tools and save or load local model files. This is expected for a scientific workflow skill but should remain user-controlled.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and file operations are explicit documentation examples, so the risk depends on user execution. They do not show hidden execution, exfiltration, or privilege escalation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign Documentation Context
low_findings.description: Network reconnaissance was triggered by the phrase inference network, and system reconnaissance maps to a benchmarking example that reads local process memory. No scanning or remote discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The theoretical-foundations line is terminology, and the workflows lines only measure the current process memory for performance benchmarking. There is no evidence of host, network, or service enumeration.

content.value_statement: Single-cell omics analysis requires careful model selection, preprocessing, and interpretation. This skill provides scvi-tools workflows for RNA, ATAC, multimodal, spatial, and differential analysis tasks.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use scVI, scANVI, totalVI, MultiVI, PeakVI, DestVI, Tangram, and related models.
Provides workflow guidance for AnnData setup, model training, latent embedding extraction, and Scanpy integration.
Supports differential expression and differential accessibility interpretation using scvi-tools model outputs.
Covers multimodal integration for CITE-seq, multiome, paired datasets, and partially paired datasets.
Documents spatial transcriptomics deconvolution and mapping workflows with reference-based models.
Includes best practices for raw counts, covariates, hyperparameters, GPU use, and model persistence.
limitations: Does not execute scvi-tools analysis or validate biological conclusions by itself.
Assumes users already have suitable AnnData objects and required Python dependencies.
Examples may need adjustment for scvi-tools version changes and dataset-specific metadata names.
Does not replace statistical review for experimental design, confounders, or publication claims.

use_cases.title: Choose a scvi-tools model for a new dataset
use_cases.description: Select an appropriate model based on modality, labels, batch structure, and analysis goals.

use_cases.title: Plan multimodal single-cell integration
use_cases.description: Design a CITE-seq or multiome workflow using totalVI, MultiVI, or related models.

use_cases.title: Interpret probabilistic differential analysis
use_cases.description: Review effect sizes, Bayes factors, FDR fields, and uncertainty estimates from scvi-tools results.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a beginner workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a basic scRNA-seq AnnData object and need a first scVI analysis plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an AnnData object with raw counts, batch labels, and cell type labels. Recommend a beginner scvi-tools workflow for batch correction, latent embeddings, clustering, and UMAP review.

prompt_templates.title: Compare model choices
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to decide between related scvi-tools models.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare scVI, scANVI, totalVI, and MultiVI for my dataset. The data includes RNA counts, protein measurements, partial labels, and multiple batches. Explain which model fits each goal.

prompt_templates.title: Design differential testing
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a defensible differential expression or accessibility analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a scvi-tools differential analysis plan for treated versus control cells. Include required metadata, group definitions, model choice, effect size thresholds, FDR interpretation, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an advanced workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: You want a critical review of a complex single-cell analysis pipeline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned scvi-tools workflow for a multi-batch spatial transcriptomics study with a scRNA-seq reference. Identify model assumptions, confounders, tuning decisions, output checks, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: User asks which model to use for CITE-seq with RNA and antibody counts.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends totalVI, explains required RNA and protein layers, and lists checks for batch covariates, protein background, latent embeddings, and normalized outputs.

output_examples.input: User asks how to compare treated and control T cells after training scVI.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines group definitions, differential expression settings, effect size thresholds, FDR review, and interpretation of uncertainty fields.

output_examples.input: User asks how to map cell types into Visium spots using a single-cell reference.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests DestVI or Stereoscope, describes reference label requirements, shared gene checks, model training stages, and spatial proportion review.

best_practices: Use raw count data and record every preprocessing step before model setup.
Register known technical covariates such as batch, donor, chemistry, and tissue source.
Validate model outputs with biological markers, batch plots, uncertainty measures, and held-out checks.
anti_patterns: Do not train scvi-tools models on log-normalized data when raw counts are required.
Do not interpret latent separation as biology before checking batch and donor effects.
Do not reuse example metadata names without matching them to the actual AnnData fields.

faq.question: What kind of data does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers single-cell RNA, ATAC, CITE-seq, multiome, spatial transcriptomics, cytometry, methylation, and related modalities supported by scvi-tools.

faq.question: Can this skill run scvi-tools code for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users run any Python commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Does it require GPU hardware?
faq.answer: No, but GPU acceleration is useful for large datasets and longer model training runs.

faq.question: Can it help with differential expression?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains scvi-tools differential testing concepts, parameters, and interpretation of model-derived results.

faq.question: Is this limited to scRNA-seq?
faq.answer: No. It also documents ATAC, multimodal, spatial, and specialized single-cell model families.

faq.question: How should users validate results?
faq.answer: Users should inspect batch mixing, marker genes, uncertainty estimates, metadata quality, and agreement with experimental design.
